title: [applyDiff_into_INVTRIG] onAnotherWorld2 [diff_P4]
description:
1-8. Inverse Functions in General

9-15. Graphing sin⁻¹(x), cos⁻¹(x), tan⁻¹(x)
    & mixing sin⁻¹(sinx) 
    - [others are shown later]

16-19. Trynna Differentiate sin⁻¹(x)
  - DTIYS 😜
  - after 18th V, 'Props of INV. FN (2of3)'
  - don't see solution before U try for 1Hour
  - Consider thinking in terms of 'ORIGINAL WORLD & INVERSE WORLD' like eddie did

20. Function vs Relations

21-26. Scaling, Shifting/Translating, Reflecting INV_TRIG_FN

27-44. More Compound, [after sin⁻¹(sinx)]
    - 27. sin⁻¹(x) + cos⁻¹(x)

45-60. moreGraphing:- Squared/Root fn
    - Powers of FN, Multiplying FN
    - Reciprocal FN / [Dividing Intro]

61-67. DIFFERENTIATION only

68. Guitar Gear
69. Count on me

70-75. [Looking Back] General Solution for NOT invTrig
76. Graphing/Viz Complicated argument [inside invTrig Fn]

77-89. Mixing more TRIG-invTrig
Thinking in Terms of Triangles
    - Try it yourself. Every Combo....
    - Before eddie reveals the answer
    - and think about the shape/Graph
    - use GeoGebra/Desmos if you want to
    - ALL COMPOUND INV_TRIG Graphs:-
    - https://www.milefoot.com/math/trig/33compositions.htm

90-98. General Solutions EXT

99-100. Looking Backwards, Once again Differentiation
